channel.title: qwertymarmot.github.io
channel.description: Sharing knowledge is the most fundamental act of friendship. Because it is a way you can give something without loosing something by Richard Stallman

item.title: Rewards are not a good motivator
item.description:
Notes:

Rewards or incentives are not good long-term motivators to get someone to perform a certain action. At first, the person will be incentivized to perform that action. However, they will not be incentivized to perform actions above and beyond said action. Because of this, rewards should not be given when creative thinking is required. If the action is desired to continue, then the rewards have to continue to be provided or have to be increased for the same level of incentive. Likewise, if someone performs an action that you consider good or doing the right thing and you reward that action, then you change the motivation for performing the active from intrinsic to extrinsic.

Source:

  • Pink, D. H. (2011). Drive: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us. Riverhead Books.
